title: What is DNA?: MedlinePlus Genetics
text:
property of DNA is that it can replicate, or make copies of itself. Each strand of DNA in the double helix can serve as a pattern for duplicating the sequence of bases. This is critical when cells divide because each new cell needs to have an exact copy of the DNA present in the old cell.
DNA is a double helix formed by base pairs attached to a sugar-phosphate backbone.
Credit: U.S. National Library of Medicine 
For more information about DNA: 
The National Human Genome Research Institute (NHGRI) provides a fact sheet on Deoxyribonucleic Acid (DNA) . 
The NHGRI Talking Glossary of Genomic and Genetic Terms offers information on DNA and mitochondrial DNA . 
StatedClearly offers a video : What is DNA and How Does it Work? 
A basic explanation and illustration of DNA can be found on the Arizona State University "Ask a Biologist" website.
